operations.name: Decide-to-pin
operations.trigger: User asks how to make an agent respect project style, OR same correction has been typed in chat >= 2 times, OR code review caught project-specific knowledge an agent should have had
operations.action: Run Phase 0 decision tree: (Q1) >=3 future sessions on this codebase? (Q2) is the rule already encoded in config files like pyproject/eslintrc/editorconfig? (Q3) is the rule guidance or must-hold invariant?
evidence: code.claude.com/docs/en/memory: 'Add to it when Claude makes the same mistake a second time'
developer.upsun.com/posts/ai/agents-md-less-is-more: context files hurt when they duplicate already-discoverable info
code.claude.com/docs/en/memory: 'CLAUDE.md instructions ... are not a hard enforcement layer'

operations.name: Write first cut
operations.action: Fill the 6-section template: Language & versions / Libraries prefer-or-avoid / Style / Layout / Workflow / Conflict-resolution clause. Total 5-15 bullets. Every bullet must be (a) concrete (verifiable from a code snippet), (b) NOT already in pyproject/package.json/eslintrc, (c) command-first or rule-first (no prose paragraphs).
operations.output: Conventions file at canonical path for the primary tool (CONVENTIONS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor/rules/main.mdc / .clinerules/coding.md / agent backstory string)
evidence: aider.chat/docs/usage/conventions.html: minimal example of 2 bullets (httpx + types)
code.claude.com/docs/en/memory: < 200 lines per CLAUDE.md, specific over vague

operations.name: Wire load mechanism
operations.action: Per tool: Aider -> add `read: CONVENTIONS.md` to `.aider.conf.yml`; Claude Code -> place at `./CLAUDE.md` or `./.claude/CLAUDE.md` (auto-loaded); Cursor -> add `.cursor/rules/main.mdc` with `alwaysApply: true` frontmatter; Cline -> place in `.clinerules/` (auto-loaded); CrewAI -> inject into each agent's `backstory=` field at construction. If multi-tool repo: make AGENTS.md canonical, have CLAUDE.md `@AGENTS.md` import, etc.
operations.output: File appears in the tool's loaded-files introspection (/tokens for Aider, /memory for Claude Code, sidebar for Cursor/Cline)

operations.name: Verify load with an A/B test
operations.action: Pick one specific bullet (e.g. 'use httpx not requests'). Ask the agent to write a new function that triggers it. Confirm the rule was followed. If not, re-check loading (the file is shown in /tokens or /memory but not influencing output -> token budget / conflicting nearby example / rule too vague / contradicting user-layer rule).
operations.output: Pass/fail. If fail, return to OP-3 with diagnosis.
evidence: aider.chat/docs/usage/conventions.html: documented A/B between with-CONVENTIONS and without

operations.name: Add a rule
operations.trigger: Same mistake twice in a session OR across sessions; OR code review caught project-specific knowledge the agent should have known; OR you re-typed the same correction in chat a third time
operations.action: Add one bullet that (a) is concrete and verifiable, (b) is not already encoded in lint/config files, (c) gives a counter-example if the rule is non-obvious. Keep file under 200 lines; if approaching, queue OP-7.
operations.output: Updated conventions file with one more bullet
evidence: code.claude.com/docs/en/memory: 'Add to it when Claude makes the same mistake a second time' (canonical trigger list)

operations.name: Delete a stale rule
operations.trigger: Codebase has moved past it (rule says X, every recent file uses Y); OR lint/CI now enforces it; OR rule is too vague to verify and has produced no measurable behavior change; OR file > 200 lines and this rule has the lowest hit rate
operations.action: Remove the bullet. If the rule now lives in lint/CI, note the migration in the commit message so future readers don't re-add it.
operations.output: Slimmer file; commit message documenting where the rule moved (if anywhere)
evidence: code.claude.com/docs/en/memory: 'Review your CLAUDE.md files ... periodically to remove outdated or conflicting instructions'

operations.name: Split into glob-scoped rule
operations.trigger: A rule applies only to a subset of the codebase (e.g. only `src/api/`); AND main file is approaching 200 lines
operations.action: Move the rule to a per-glob file with `paths:` frontmatter (Claude Code `.claude/rules/api.md`; Cursor `.mdc` with `globs:` field; Cline `.clinerules/api.md` with `paths:` frontmatter). Main file shrinks; scoped rule loads only when the agent touches matching files.
operations.output: New scoped rule file; main file shrunk to baseline.
evidence: cursor.com/docs/rules: `globs:` in .mdc frontmatter, nested .cursor/rules per subdir

operations.name: Cross-tool unification
operations.trigger: Project is used with multiple coder-tools (e.g. Claude Code + Cursor + Aider on the same repo)
operations.action: Designate `AGENTS.md` at repo root as canonical (~80 lines, the actual rules). Have CLAUDE.md be a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import. For Cursor's `.mdc`, either duplicate (acceptable if AGENTS.md is small) or generate via pre-commit hook. For Cline, symlink `.clinerules/main.md` -> `../AGENTS.md`. For Aider, set `read: AGENTS.md` in `.aider.conf.yml`. Note: Claude Code's `/init` reads existing `.cursorrules` and `.windsurfrules` automatically, so a legacy `.cursorrules` is not a blocker.
operations.output: One canonical AGENTS.md; thin per-tool pointers/imports/symlinks.

anti_patterns_summary: AP-1: Project narrative in conventions file (zero behavior change, costs tokens)
AP-2: Duplicating package.json / pyproject.toml / eslintrc content the agent already reads
AP-3: Ambiguous directives ('be careful', 'follow best practices')
AP-4: Contradictory rules across user-layer + project-layer + ancestor
AP-5: TODO lists / roadmaps inside conventions
AP-6: Pinning conventions before the codebase has stabilised (greenfield)
AP-7: Using conventions as enforcement (use hooks/lint/CI for must-hold rules)
AP-8: File > 200 lines without splitting into glob-scoped rules
AP-9: 30 micro-files in .cursor/rules/ — the opposite extreme; aim for 3-8 files when splitting
AP-10: Forgetting to load the file (Aider --read not in .aider.conf.yml; Cursor alwaysApply: false on a rule meant to always apply)

key_thresholds.claude_code_per_file: 200 lines per CLAUDE.md (full file always loads)
key_thresholds.claude_code_memory_md_truncation: 200 lines or 25KB of MEMORY.md (auto-memory only; not CLAUDE.md)

load_introspection_commands.crewai: inspect agent.backstory or agent.system_prompt in Python
